widgets_values: cinematic 3D animated video, production quality, detailed material rendering, stable identity and consistent character appearance. same face and same identity as the first frame, preserve clothing, lighting, scene layout, material detail, fabric texture, sofa texture, hair and skin shading. main audio drives speech and lip sync, natural face animation, clear mouth motion, no identity drift. camera is stable unless a local segment explicitly asks for camera movement

nodes.title: Shezw Director-Pro Notes
widgets_values:
# Shezw Director-Pro: real LTX Director / Prompt Relay workflow

This workflow is based on WhatDreamsCost-ComfyUI LTX Director.

The important part is the LTXDirector node:
- `global_prompt` holds persistent scene, identity, style, and material rules.
- `timeline_data` stores visual timeline blocks.
- `local_prompts` stores one action prompt per time segment.
- `segment_lengths` stores pixel-frame duration per action segment.

LTXDirector does not simply concatenate text. It tokenizes global + local prompts, maps token ranges, then patches LTX cross-attention with Prompt Relay temporal masks. That is the mechanism that makes action descriptions reach the model at the correct time.

Edit action in timeline blocks or in local prompts. Keep each local prompt action-focused: expression, mouth movement, hand gesture, body pose, camera. Avoid stuffing scene/style into every action block.
